Condamne toutes les violations des règles applicables du  droit international, notamment du droit international humanitaire, et toutes les violations du droit international des droits de l'homme et atteintes à ces droits, commises par toutes  les  parties  au  conflit,  en  particulier  à  l'encontre  des  enfants, prie  avec insistance toutes les parties au conflit de mettre en œuvre les mesures prescrites dans les Conclusions sur le sort des enfants touchés par le conflit armé au Soudan du Sud adoptées le 8 mai 2015 par le Groupe de travail du Conseil de sécurité sur le sort des enfants  en  temps  de  conflit  armé,  notamment  de  libérer  tous  les  enfants  qui combattent dans leurs rangs, se félicite que des groupes aient récemment libéré des enfants, demande à tous les groupes armés de libérer rapidement d'autres enfants qui se trouvent dans leurs rangs, demande instamment au  Gouvernement sud-soudanais de  mettre  en  œuvre  intégralement  et  immédiatement  toutes  les  dispositions  de l'accord de renouvellement des engagements relatifs au plan d'action révisé visant à faire  cesser  et  à  prévenir  le  recrutement  et  l'utilisation  d'enfants  dans  les  conflits armés  et  autres  violations,  signé  avec  l'ONU  le  24 juin  2014,  et exhorte l'Armée populaire de libération du Soudan dans l'opposition à mettre en œuvre intégralement et immédiatement son plan d'action visant à faire cesser et à prévenir le recrutement et  l'utilisation  d'enfants  ainsi  que  le  meurtre  ou  l'atteinte  à  l'intégrité  physique d'enfants,  signé  avec  l'ONU  le  7 janvier  2016,  et souligne qu'il  importe  que  le
items_en: (i) To use its good offices to support the peace process, in particular the high-level forum for the revitalization of the Agreement;

(ii) To participate in the work of the Ceasefire Monitoring Mechanism and the monitoring of the implementation of the transitional security arrangements for the implementation of its mandate to monitor and monitor the implementation of the ceasefire, including by facilitating the work of the Ceasefire Monitoring Mechanism and assisting it in identifying and collecting information on the violence committed and ensuring, in general, that it effectively identifies the perpetrators of such violence;
(iii) To participate and participate actively in the work of the Joint Monitoring and Evaluation Commission;

8. Stresses that, in the long term, the viability of the peace process cannot be achieved without full support from all parties, urges all parties to participate in good faith in the peace process in order to achieve the necessary compromises for peace and stability in South Sudan, reminds the parties that UNMISS will carry out only the tasks related to the peace process set out in paragraph 7 (d), and notes its intention to continue to actively review the tasks and composition of UNMISS, taking into account the possible results of the high-level forum for revitalization;

9 . Decides that, in order to ensure, in cooperation with the Interim Government of National Unity, the safety and security of the South Sudanese population and to establish an enabling environment for the implementation of the Agreement, UNMISS will continue to include a regional protection force, recalls that, as provided for in resolution 2304 (2016), UNMISS is mandated to provide a safe environment in Juba and surrounding areas and other parts of South Sudan, as appropriate, and authorizes UNMISS to use all necessary means, including through decisive action, as appropriate, and through active patrols, to fulfil its mandate, in order to:

(i) To promote conditions conducive to ensuring free movement, within, outside and around Juba, by protecting the city's entry and exit modes and the main routes of communication and transport within Juba;
(ii) To protect the airport for its continued operation and the key facilities in Juba, essential to the well-being of its people, designated by the Special Representative of the Secretary-General;

(iii) To combat promptly and effectively any actor who, according to credible information, prepares or conducts attacks against United Nations civilian protection sites, other United Nations premises, United Nations personnel, international and national humanitarian actors, or civilians;

10. Affirms that it is crucial for UNMISS to be able to use all its bases without restrictions, including but not limited to its Tomping base, in the implementation of its mandate, and in this regard calls upon the Government of South Sudan to comply with its obligation under the Status of Forces Agreement to ensure unimpeded access to United Nations premises;

11. Recalls its resolution 2086 (2013), and reaffirms the fundamental principles of peacekeeping, as set out in the statement of its President contained in document S/PRST/2015/22, namely, the consent of the parties, impartiality and non-use of force, except in self-defence or the defence of the mandate, and recognizes that the mandate of each peace mission is determined according to the needs and circumstances of the country concerned and that it expects the full implementation of the mandates authorized by it;

12. Stresses that the mandate of UNMISS set out in paragraphs 7 and 9 of this resolution includes the authority to use all means necessary to protect United Nations personnel, facilities and equipment for the purpose of defusing violence, including through preventive deployment and active patrols, including through regular interaction with the civilian population, to protect civilians from threats wherever they arise, to create conditions conducive to the delivery of humanitarian assistance by international and national actors, and to support the implementation of the Agreement and the peace process, and stresses that such provisions include, within UNMISS capabilities and areas, the defence of civilian protection sites, the establishment of sites around areas that are not used for hostile purposes by any force, including the extension of the existing civilian protection sites to the Mission's civilian protection sites, the provision of such facilities, the provision of such facilities, including, within the limits of UNMISS capabilities and areas, the provision of protection sites for civilians, the establishment of sites around which are not used for hostile purposes, including by extending the existing civilian protection sites of the Mission, the provision of access to, the provision of access to, and the provision of access to, armed security sites, and the provision of access to, the use of security facilities, and security, including the provision of security, including the provision of facilities, the provision of facilities, the provision of security, including the provision of security, including the provision of security, the provision of security of security, the provision of facilities, the provision of security, the provision of security, the provision of security, the provision of the provision of the security,

13. Invites and encourages the Special Representative of the Secretary-General to lead the operations of an integrated UNMISS, to coordinate all activities of the United Nations system in the Republic of South Sudan and to exercise his good offices by playing a leading role in assisting the entities of the United Nations system in South Sudan to the Joint Monitoring and Evaluation Commission, the African Union, IGAD and other stakeholders, as well as the parties, in the implementation of the Agreement and the promotion of peace and reconciliation, stresses in this regard the central role of the Ceasefire Monitoring and Monitoring Mechanism and the monitoring of the implementation of the transitional security arrangements, as well as the importance of the assistance provided by UNMISS in the implementation of its mandate, and in this regard reaffirms the crucial role of the United Nations in coordinating with regional organizations and other actors in promoting political dialogue between the parties, contributing to the cessation of hostilities and bringing the parties to an inclusive peace process;

14. Requests UNMISS to give due consideration in all aspects of its mandate to the cross-cutting gender issue, reaffirms the importance of appropriate gender training and expertise in all missions mandated by the Security Council, and encourages troop- and police-contributing countries to take steps to deploy more women to the military and civilian components of the Mission and its police component;

15. Calls upon UNMISS to continue to increase its presence and patrol more actively in areas at risk of high conflict and concentration of internally displaced persons and refugees, including through the implementation of its early warning strategy, in all areas and on major population movement routes, to extend its presence, including through systematic deployment and patrols, to areas of displacement, return, resettlement and reintegration to create security conditions conducive to the safe and voluntary return of internally displaced persons and refugees, and to review periodically its geographical deployment to ensure that its forces are in the best position to carry out its mission;

16. Recognizes that the effective implementation of the mandate of UNMISS is the responsibility of all stakeholders and depends on a number of key factors, including whether the mandate is well defined, realistic and achievable; the political will, effectiveness and accountability of leaders at all levels; the adequacy of resources; policy, planning and operational guidelines and training; also recognizes that the measurement and monitoring of peacekeeping results should be based on comprehensive and objective methods based on clear and well-defined criteria; welcomes in this regard the initiatives of the Secretary-General to establish a culture of effectiveness in all United Nations peacekeeping operations that implements the Policy on Verification and Improvement of Operational Preparedness, to conduct performance assessments of United Nations uniformed personnel and civilian personnel, and to use the Peacekeeping Preparedness System to ensure results-based decisions on the deployment of peacekeepers, and requests the Secretary-General to continue his efforts in this regard;

17. Recalls the statement of its President S/PRST/2015/22 and its resolution 2272 (2016), and requests the Secretary-General to take all necessary measures to ensure that UNMISS fully complies with the United Nations zero-tolerance policy on sexual exploitation and abuse and to ensure that the background of all Mission personnel is verified in order to ensure that they have not engaged in any sexual misconduct while in the service of the United Nations and to keep the Mission informed of the progress made in this regard in its reports to the General Assembly, and urges troop- and police-contributing countries to take appropriate preventive measures, including predeployment awareness training, and to ensure that members of their contingents who commit such acts are held fully accountable;

18. Requests UNMISS to ensure that any support provided to non-United Nations security forces is strictly consistent with the human rights due diligence policy in the event of United Nations support to non-United Nations security forces;

19. Also requests UNMISS to assist, within existing resources, the Committee established pursuant to paragraph 16 of resolution 2206 (2015) and the Panel of Experts established pursuant to the same resolution, urges all parties and all Member States, as well as international, regional and subregional organizations, to cooperate with the Panel of Experts, and urges all Member States concerned to ensure the safety of the members of the Panel of Experts and to give them free access to persons, documents and sites to enable the Panel to fulfil its mandate;

20. Condemns in the strongest terms all attacks and threats against UNMISS personnel and United Nations facilities, as well as against IGAD personnel and facilities, stresses that such attacks may constitute violations of the Agreement on the Status of War Forces or War Crimes, requires all parties to respect the inviolability of United Nations premises and to refrain immediately from any violence against those gathered therein, reaffirms that the Interim Government of National Unity is bound by the provisions of the Agreement and demands the immediate and safe release of the abducted and detained United Nations and associated personnel;

21. Condemns the clashes that took place in Malakal in February 2016 and the fighting in Juba in July 2016, and urges the United Nations to take into account the lessons learned so as to enhance the capacity of UNMISS to carry out its mandate, in particular with regard to the protection of civilians, as well as to improve the UNMISS chain of command, increase the effectiveness of its operations, enhance the safety and security of its personnel and enhance the capacity of UNMISS to manage complex situations;
22. Reiterates its request to UNMISS to continue to take measures, as appropriate, to ensure the safety of its air operations in South Sudan and to report thereon to the General Assembly;

23. Condemns in the strongest terms the attacks on humanitarian assistance and the looting of humanitarian relief, including food and medicine, as well as the looting of facilities such as hospitals, medical facilities and warehouses, requires the parties to allow, in accordance with the applicable provisions of international law and the guiding principles of the United Nations on emergency humanitarian assistance, namely, humanity, impartiality, neutrality and independence, prompt, safe and free access to relief personnel, equipment and supplies, and the prompt delivery of humanitarian assistance to all those in need throughout South Sudan, in particular internally displaced persons and refugees, stresses the obligation to respect and protect all medical personnel and humanitarian workers whose activities are exclusively medical, their means of transport and equipment, as well as hospitals and other medical facilities, stresses also that any return must be voluntary, knowingly and with dignity and security, and that the same applies to all other durable solutions concerning internally displaced persons and their rights to respect the rights of civilians and to respect the rights of the displaced and to respect the rights of the refugees and to respect for the rights of the refugees and to respect for the rights of the refugees and to respect for the rights of the refugees;

24. Demands that all parties immediately cease all forms of violence, violations of and violations of human rights and violations of international humanitarian law, including rape and other forms of sexual and gender-based violence, and that perpetrators of such acts be held accountable in order to break the current cycle of impunity;

25. Condemns all violations of the applicable rules of international law, including international humanitarian law, and all violations of and violations of international human rights law, committed by all parties to the conflict, in particular against children, urges all parties to the conflict to implement the measures prescribed in the Conclusions on the fate of children affected by armed conflict in South Sudan adopted by the Security Council Working Group on Children and Armed Conflict on 8 May 2015, including the release of all children fighting in their ranks, welcomes the recent release of groups from children, calls upon all armed groups to release rapidly other children in their ranks, urges the Government of South Sudan to implement fully and immediately all provisions of the agreement to renew the commitments to the revised plan of action to stop and prevent the recruitment and use of children in armed conflict and other violations, signed with the United Nations on 24 June 2014, and urges the Sudan People's Liberation Army signed with the opposition to ensure the full and immediate implementation of the United Nations plan of action and to prevent the full and immediate use of children in armed conflict and other violations, and urges the Sudan People's Liberation Army to implement all provisions of the agreement on the renewal of commitments relating to the revised plan of action to halt and the recruitment of children, and the immediate cessation of the United Nations plan of action, and the use of

text_fr: Gouvernement  sud-soudanais  fournisse  rapidement  aux  enfants  touchés  par  les conflits armés une aide appropriée à la réinsertion et à la réadaptation tout en veillant à  répondre  aux  besoins  particuliers  des  filles  et  des  enfants  handicapés,  tels  que l'accès aux services de santé, à un soutien psychosocial et à des programmes éducatifs qui  contribuent  au  bien-être  des  enfants  et  à  une  paix  et  une  sécurité  durables,  et demande à la communauté internationale d'apporter son soutien à ces efforts ;
text_en: Government of South Sudan provides timely and adequate reintegration and rehabilitation assistance to children affected by armed conflict, while addressing the special needs of girls and children with disabilities, such as access to health services, psychosocial support and educational programmes that contribute to the well-being of children and sustainable peace and security, and calls upon the international community to support these efforts;

items_fr: 26. Engage vivement l'APLS, l'APLS dans l'opposition et les autres groupes armés  à  empêcher  que  de  nouveaux  actes  de  violence  sexuelle  soient  commis, demande instamment au Gouvernement provisoire d'union nationale et à l'APLS dans l'opposition  de  donner  effet  aux  engagements  et  plans  d'action  qu'ils  ont  adoptés conjointement et unilatéralement en vue de la prévention de la violence sexuelle liée au conflit, qui mettent l'accent sur la prévention, la responsabilité et le renforcement de l'assistance aux victimes, engage vivement les dirigeants de l'APLS à donner des ordres exprès concernant la prévention de la violence sexuelle liée au conflit, et exige du  Gouvernement  provisoire d'union nationale qu'il  prenne des  mesures  concrètes pour que les membres de ses forces qui commettent des actes de violence sexuelle en soient tenus responsables ;

27. Insiste sur  le  fait  que  la  recherche  de  la  vérité  et  la  réconciliation  sont essentielles pour instaurer la paix au Soudan du Sud et, à cet égard, souligne que la Commission vérité, réconciliation et apaisement a un rôle primordial à jouer dans la consolidation  de  la  paix  au  Soudan  du  Sud,  comme  indiqué  dans  l'Accord,  en conduisant les  efforts  visant  à  parvenir  à  la  cohésion  nationale  et  à  promouvoir  la paix, la réconciliation nationale et l'apaisement ;

28. Prend note des mesures prises par l'Union africaine en vue de la mise en place du Tribunal mixte pour le Soudan du Sud prévu par le chapitre V de l'Accord et de l'action menée à ce jour par l'Organisation des Nations Unies, se félicite que l'Union  africaine  ait  officiellement  demandé  à  l'Organisation  de  lui  apporter  une assistance technique en vue de la mise en place du Tribunal mixte et prie le Secrétaire général de continuer à fournir une assistance technique à la Commission de l'Union africaine et au Gouvernement provisoire d'union nationale pour la mise en place du Tribunal mixte pour le Soudan du Sud et l'application des autres parties du chapitre V de l'Accord, notamment en ce qui concerne l'établissement de la Commission vérité, réconciliation et apaisement ;

29. Prend note du communiqué du Conseil de paix et de sécurité de l'Union africaine du 8 février 2018 et à cet égard demande au Gouvernement sud-soudanais de signer sans plus attendre le Mémorandum d'accord portant création du Tribunal mixte pour le Soudan du Sud conclu avec l'Union africaine, et prie la communauté internationale de prêter son soutien à la création de ce tribunal ;

30. Demande également au Gouvernement sud-soudanais de mener à bien en toute diligence et en toute transparence les enquêtes sur les allégations de violations des droits de l'homme et d'atteintes à ces droits, dans le respect de ses obligations internationales, et l' invite à publier les comptes rendus de ces enquêtes ;

31. Demande en outre au Gouvernement sud-soudanais, tout en prenant note du  paragraphe 3.2.2  du  chapitre V  de  l'Accord,  d'amener  tous  les  responsables  de violations des droits de l'homme et d'atteintes à ces droits et de violations du droit international humanitaire à répondre de leurs actes, de garantir à toutes les victimes de violences sexuelles l'égale protection de la loi et l'égal accès à la justice, et de garantir  l'égal  respect  des  droits  des  femmes  et  des  filles  à  l'occasion  de  ces procédures, et note que l'application de mesures globales de justice transitionnelle, y compris  de  mesures  visant  à  assurer  le  respect  du  principe  de  responsabilité,  à
items_en: 26. Urges SPLA, SPLA in the opposition and other armed groups to prevent further acts of sexual violence, urges the Provisional Government of National Unity and SPLA in the opposition to give effect to the commitments and plans of action they have adopted jointly and unilaterally for the prevention of conflict-related sexual violence, which emphasize the prevention, accountability and strengthening of victim assistance, urges SPLA leaders to issue explicit orders regarding the prevention of conflict-related sexual violence, and calls upon the Interim Government of National Unity to take concrete measures to hold its forces responsible for sexual violence;

27. Stresses that truth-seeking and reconciliation are essential for peace in South Sudan, and in this regard stresses that the Truth, Reconciliation and Peace Commission has a primary role to play in the consolidation of peace in South Sudan, as outlined in the Agreement, by leading efforts to achieve national cohesion and to promote peace, national reconciliation and calm;

28. Takes note of the steps taken by the African Union towards the establishment of the Joint Tribunal for South Sudan as provided for in chapter V of the Agreement and the action taken to date by the United Nations, welcomes the formal request by the African Union to the United Nations to provide technical assistance for the establishment of the Joint Tribunal, and requests the Secretary-General to continue to provide technical assistance to the African Union Commission and the Interim Government of National Union for the establishment of the Joint Tribunal for South Sudan and the implementation of other parts of chapter V of the Agreement, including the establishment of the Truth, Reconciliation and Appreciation Commission;

29. Takes note of the communiqué of the Peace and Security Council of the African Union of 8 February 2018, and in this regard calls upon the Government of South Sudan to sign without further delay the Memorandum of Understanding establishing the Joint Tribunal for South Sudan with the African Union, and requests the international community to support the establishment of this Tribunal;

30. Also calls upon the Government of South Sudan to conduct expeditiously and transparently investigations into allegations of human rights violations and violations, in compliance with its international obligations, and invites the Government to publish the reports of such investigations;

31. Further calls upon the Government of South Sudan, while taking note of chapter V, paragraph 3.2.2, of the Agreement, to hold all those responsible for human rights violations and violations of human rights and violations of international humanitarian law accountable, to ensure equal protection of the law and equal access to justice for all victims of sexual violence, and to ensure equal respect for the rights of women and girls in these proceedings, and notes that comprehensive transitional justice measures, including measures to ensure respect for the principle of accountability, should be implemented, in accordance with the relevant provisions of the Agreement, in accordance with the provisions of the Agreement.

text_en: seeking the truth and making reparations, is fundamental to achieving appeasement and reconciliation;

items_fr: 32. Prie le Secrétaire général de continuer à lui faire rapport tous les mois sur les violations de l'Accord sur le statut des forces ou sur les manœuvres d'obstruction visant la MINUSS ;

33. Prie le Secrétaire général de lui rendre compte de l'exécution du mandat de la MINUSS et des manœuvres d'obstructions qu'elle rencontre dans l'exécution de  son  mandat,  dans  un  rapport  écrit  détaillé  devant  être  soumis  dans  les  90 jours suivant l'adoption de la résolution, puis tous les 90 jours par la suite, et souligne que ce rapport devra notamment comprendre :
· Des informations sur les progrès accomplis dans la mise en œuvre de la Politique de diligence voulue ;

· Des informations précises et détaillées sur la manière dont la Mission s'acquitte de ses tâches de protection des civils, notamment, mais pas exclusivement, sur la capacité de réaction et la performance des contingents et les nouvelles zones de patrouille et les déploiements systématiques ;
· Des informations sur la prise en compte de la problématique hommes-femmes dans tous les aspects du mandat de la Mission ;
· Des informations sur la participation des femmes aux processus de paix ;
· Des informations sur les mesures prises pour améliorer  la sûreté et la sécurité du personnel de l'Organisation des Nations Unies ;
· Des  informations  sur  les  mesures  prises  pour  régler  les  problèmes  de performance recensés lors des évaluations mentionnées au paragraphe 16 ;
· Un  rapport  exhaustif  sur  les  questions  relatives  aux  droits  de  l'homme  au Soudan du Sud ;
· Des recommandations sur les mesures à prendre pour adapter la MINUSS à la situation sur le terrain ;

34. Prie le  Secrétaire  général  de  lui  rendre  compte,  dans  ses  rapports trimestriels,  de  l'assistance  technique  fournie  en  application  du  paragraphe 28 ci-dessus, invite l'Union africaine à communiquer au Secrétaire général, aux fins de l'établissement  de  son  rapport,  des  informations  sur  les  progrès  accomplis  dans  la mise  en  place  du  Tribunal  mixte  pour  le  Soudan  du  Sud,  et déclare son  intention d'évaluer, lorsqu'il recevra les rapports du Secrétaire général, le travail accompli en vue de l'établissement du Tribunal mixte, conformément aux normes internationales ;
35. Décide de rester saisi de la question.
items_en: 32. Requests the Secretary-General to continue to report to the Council on a monthly basis on violations of the Status of Forces Agreement or obstructions against UNMISS;

33. Requests the Secretary-General to report to the Council on the implementation of the mandate of UNMISS and its obstructions in the implementation of its mandate, in a detailed written report to be submitted within 90 days of the adoption of the resolution, and every 90 days thereafter, and stresses that the report should include, inter alia:
· Information on progress in the implementation of the Due Diligence Policy;

· Accurate and detailed information on how the Mission carries out its civilian protection tasks, including, but not limited to, the response capacity and performance of contingents and new patrol areas and systematic deployments;
· Information on gender mainstreaming in all aspects of the Mission ' s mandate;
· Information on women ' s participation in peace processes;
· Information on measures taken to improve the safety and security of United Nations personnel;
· Information on measures taken to address the performance issues identified in the evaluations referred to in paragraph 16;
· A comprehensive report on human rights issues in South Sudan;
· Recommendations on measures to be taken to adapt UNMISS to the situation on the ground;

34. Requests the Secretary-General to report, in his quarterly reports, on the technical assistance provided pursuant to paragraph 28 above, invites the African Union to provide the Secretary-General with information on the progress made in the establishment of the Joint Tribunal for South Sudan for the preparation of his report, and expresses its intention to assess, when receiving the reports of the Secretary-General, the work done towards the establishment of the Joint Tribunal in accordance with international standards;
35. Decides to remain seized of the matter.
